name: Billy Eckstine
abstract: William Clarence Eckstine (July 8, 1914 – March 8, 1993) was an American jazz and pop singer and a bandleader during the swing and bebop eras. He was noted for his rich, almost operatic bass-baritone voice.

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: So I said, “well I’ll play bass in the band, that’s fine with me.” So I got to play with Billy Eckstine and the drummer was Charlie Persip.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: In fact, Earl Hines’s band, I had met Billy Eckstine, Budd Johnson, Pee Wee Jackson...
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: with the exceptions of the greats Ella, Sarah, Joe Williams, Billy Eckstine...
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Brunious
about_quotes.evidence: You know my dad always was a great musician, wrote for Billy Eckstine...
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Brunious interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ruth Brown
about_quotes.evidence: I toured with Count Basie, George Shearing, Billy Eckstine as a unit.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruth Brown interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: Billy Eckstine, the headliner with Bobby Tucker played piano, and Sarah Vaughan and Roy Haynes and Jimmy Jones on piano... For two months, can you do better than that if you want to play with people?
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]
